explanation.summary: Carbs are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in A tier with an overall score of 51 from a base nutrition score of 51. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: practical staple carb source
topPros.explanation: Tubers are often judged by how usable they are day to day.

topPros.title: whole-food carb package
topPros.explanation: That matters because it is a cleaner baseline than heavily refined carbs.

topPros.title: can be quite filling
topPros.explanation: A useful practical strength when the preparation stays sensible.

topCons.title: still a carb-heavy category
topCons.explanation: That is the core ceiling tubers have to manage.

topCons.title: preparation changes outcomes a lot
topCons.explanation: What you do to a tuber can make or break it.

topCons.title: protein support is weak
topCons.explanation: Tubers still need help from the rest of the meal.

closing.summary: Yam stands out for practical staple carb source and whole-food carb package. but it also gets held back by still a carb-heavy category and preparation changes outcomes a lot. Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.
